title: Fulbright English Teaching Assistant Award to Bahrain
description: U.S. citizens with a bachelor's degree teach English 20-25 hours per week alongside faculty at Bahraini universities for an academic-year grant (September-June), while serving as cultural ambassadors. The award is part of the U.S. Department of State's Fulbright U.S. Student Program, administered with Bahraini host institutions and the U.S. Embassy in Manama. Benefits include a monthly living stipend, health insurance, and round-trip travel to Bahrain. (No other real, verifiable, non-aggregator opportunity into Bahrain for this audience could be confirmed after extensive search, so this is used as a last resort for this one country.)
org: Fulbright U.S. Student Program (U.S. Department of State, Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs)
visaNote: Grantees receive a Bahraini residence/exchange status arranged with Fulbright Commission and host-institution support; this is not an independent work visa.

title: Critical Language Scholarship - Persian Program in Dushanbe, Tajikistan
description: A fully funded, roughly eight-week intensive Persian-language and cultural immersion summer institute held in Dushanbe, Tajikistan, hosted by American Councils for International Education since 2010. Participants complete about one academic year of Persian coursework, take introductory Tajik and Cyrillic-script lessons, and live with host families. The program covers international airfare, tuition, housing, and meals for U.S. undergraduate and graduate students; roughly 500 are selected annually from over 5,000 applicants.
org: U.S. Department of State Critical Language Scholarship (CLS) Program
visaNote: Participants receive program-arranged entry/visa status for Tajikistan through American Councils; no independent work authorization is involved.

title: MEXT Scholarship for Research Students (Graduate Studies in Japan)
description: Japan's Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT) funds international graduate applicants to conduct research at a Japanese university, typically as a pathway into a master's or doctoral program. The award covers tuition, a monthly stipend the program lists at roughly ¥143,000-145,000, and round-trip international airfare. Recipients apply through the embassy-recommendation route via their local Japanese embassy or consulate, since embassy deadlines vary by country.
org: Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT), Government of Japan
visaNote: Recipients receive a Japanese student ('College Student') visa arranged through the embassy; this is not a work visa, though limited part-time work permission can be obtained separately.

title: Singapore International Graduate Award (SINGA)
description: A*STAR's flagship PhD scholarship funds international students to pursue doctoral research in science and engineering at the National University of Singapore, Nanyang Technological University, the Singapore University of Technology and Design, or an A*STAR research institute. The award covers full tuition, a monthly stipend the program lists at about S$2,700-3,200, a one-time settlement allowance, and an airfare grant, for up to four years. Applicants must not be Singapore citizens or permanent residents and must not have studied in Singapore before.
org: Agency for Science, Technology and Research (A*STAR), Singapore
visaNote: Awardees receive a Singapore student pass arranged by the host university; not a work visa.

title: Thailand International Postgraduate Programme (TIPP) Scholarship
description: TICA's flagship scholarship funds nationals of eligible developing countries to complete a master's degree at a Thai partner university under themes such as public health, climate change and environment, agriculture and food security, the Bio-Circular-Green Economy model, and the Sufficiency Economy Philosophy. Benefits include tuition, a monthly living and accommodation allowance, and a return economy air ticket. Candidates must be nominated by their country's National Focal Point for international development cooperation.
org: Thailand International Cooperation Agency (TICA), Royal Thai Ministry of Foreign Affairs
visaNote: A Thai education (ED) visa is arranged through the host university and TICA; not a work visa.

title: Malaysia International Scholarship (MIS)
description: The Ministry of Higher Education's Malaysia International Scholarship funds outstanding international graduates to pursue full-time master's or PhD study at Malaysian public universities and selected private institutions. It covers tuition fees and a monthly living allowance the ministry lists at RM1,500; master's applicants must be under 40 and PhD applicants under 45 at the time of application. Applications are submitted through MOHE's online scholarship portal on an annual cycle (the 2026/2027 round has already closed and moved to shortlisting).
visaNote: A Malaysian student pass is arranged through the host university; this is not a work visa.

title: Darmasiswa RI Scholarship Programme
description: Darmasiswa is the Indonesian government's scholarship for foreign nationals to study Indonesian language, arts, or culture for one year at partner universities across Indonesia. It provides a monthly living allowance the ministry lists at IDR 4,700,000 plus one-time book and settlement allowances, though participants cover their own international airfare. Applicants must be 18-32 years old, hold at least a high school diploma, and be nationals of a country with diplomatic relations with Indonesia.
org: Ministry of Education, Culture, Research, and Technology of the Republic of Indonesia
visaNote: A social-cultural/student visa sponsored by the host university is required; this is not a work visa.

title: BRAC BRIDGE Internship Programme
description: A six-month, paid internship with BRAC, one of the world's largest development NGOs, based mainly in Dhaka with field visits to project sites across Bangladesh. Interns contribute to research, reporting, and fieldwork within an assigned department; BRAC explicitly accepts international applicants, English is the working language, and Bangla is not required (translation support is available). The internship is paid with a monthly stipend, though international interns arrange their own accommodation and visa, and applications are accepted on a rolling basis.
visaNote: International interns are responsible for securing the appropriate Bangladeshi visa; BRAC provides guidance but the internship is not described as visa-sponsored.

title: ANHS Himalayan Studies Fellowship
description: The Association for Nepal and Himalayan Studies awards a $4,500 fellowship, funded from its own operating budget, to support short-term research or scholarly projects in Nepal and the wider Himalayan region. Unlike ANHS's CAORC-funded fellowships (which are U.S.-citizens-only), this award is open to pre-PhD students and early-career scholars of any citizenship, with priority given to scholars from the region. Applicants must be active ANHS members or affiliated with an ANHS institutional member.
org: Association for Nepal and Himalayan Studies (ANHS)
visaNote: A Nepali tourist or research visa is typically used for short-term fieldwork; this is not a work visa.
